description: Public calibration corpora for the Verify v1 receipt product. Each entry pins a third-party repository at a specific commit; calibration measurements in attempts.jsonl reference these corpora by id.

corpora.description: cal.com GitHub Actions workflow corpus for GHA-SHA-PIN-01 measurement. End-user TS scheduling app with high-density unpinned third-party action references. Pre-survey 2026-04-29: 50 workflow files, 194 uses: declarations, only 7 SHA-pinned (~187 candidate findings predicted).

sources.name: cal.com
sources.notes: TS-application repo. Workflow density predicts ~187 candidate findings clearing 10-floor by >18×.

suitability.suitability_reason: First corpus for GHA-SHA-PIN-01. High density of tag-pinned third-party action references; structurally distinct from argo-cd/kubernetes (TS application code, not infrastructure tooling).

limitations: Workflow corpus only — does not include the repo's main TS source. Reusable workflow callers may use local references; v1 detector excludes local at fire-time.

corpora.description: trigger.dev GitHub Actions workflow corpus for GHA-SHA-PIN-01 measurement. Companion corpus to gha-calcom-v1. Pre-survey 2026-04-29: 21 workflow files, 115 uses: declarations, only 2 SHA-pinned (~113 candidate findings predicted).

sources.name: trigger.dev
sources.notes: TS-application repo (background-jobs platform). Workflow density predicts ~113 candidate findings clearing 10-floor by >11×.

suitability.suitability_reason: Second corpus for GHA-SHA-PIN-01. Pairs with gha-calcom-v1 for two-corpus standard-path bar evaluation. Both are TS-application repos but represent distinct projects with independent CI/CD hygiene practices.

corpora.description: apache/airflow Dockerfile corpus for CONTAINER-ROOT-01 v2 measurement. Replaces iac-kubernetes-v1 (held v1 on private-registry AMB.c saturation) as the v2 second corpus. Apache Airflow is end-user workflow infrastructure that ships in containers; production Dockerfiles use public-base images (python, openjdk) heavily — different shape from container-tooling repos. 21 Dockerfiles at SHA 57955085ecfbd92567ac899406dc4269f966635d (cloned 2026-04-29).

sources.name: airflow
sources.scope: files matching Dockerfile or Dockerfile.* anywhere in repo

suitability.suitability_reason: v2 second corpus replacing iac-kubernetes-v1. End-user OSS workflow application (not container-tooling source). Public-base images (python, openjdk) dominate. 21 Dockerfiles clear the 10-finding sample floor with >2x headroom assuming reasonable production-context-pass rate.

limitations: Airflow's Dockerfile.ci variant tests §2.4 filename-suffix filter.

corpora.description: argo-cd YAML manifest corpus for K8S-MISSING-LIMITS-01 measurement. End-user GitOps controller with substantial in-repo k8s manifest density. Pre-survey 2026-04-29: 31 production-context workload manifests (Pod/Deployment/StatefulSet/DaemonSet/Job/CronJob), distinct from kubernetes/kubernetes' build-tooling-heavy distribution.

sources.name: argo-cd
sources.notes: Sparse-checkout YAML-only. 31 production workload manifests after §2.4 production-context filter.

suitability.suitability_reason: Primary corpus for multiple operator-deferred-decision k8s shapes. 31 production manifests after §2.4 filter; argo-cd manifests/ tree ships unbounded by design — every fire is a genuine TP under operator-deferred-decision predicates. 367 non-templated container-bearing documents and 178 Pod-template kinds. For K8S-IMAGE-TAG-LATEST-01: known FP.b cluster at resource_customizations/*/health_test.yaml (49 raw matches) which pre-reg explicitly enumerates.

limitations: Sparse-clone YAML-only. argo-cd's manifest paths under manifests/ and install/ are production-context.

corpora.description: grafana/grafana Dockerfile corpus for CONTAINER-ROOT-01 measurement. Second IaC corpus, structurally distinct from kubernetes (different organization, different build-tooling conventions). 30 Dockerfiles at SHA 81b2e8450179046c2d4e94b19922cbdd88c8ec61 (cloned 2026-04-29).

sources.name: grafana
sources.scope: files matching Dockerfile or Dockerfile.* anywhere in repo

suitability.suitability_reason: Second CONTAINER-ROOT-01 corpus. Independent organization from kubernetes; different conventions, different production-vs-build mix. 30 Dockerfiles clear the 10-finding sample floor with >2x headroom.

limitations: Grafana uses some Dockerfile.* variants (Dockerfile.dev, Dockerfile.ubuntu) that test §2.1 source-format rules and §2.4 production-context filter.

corpora.description: kubernetes/kubernetes YAML manifest corpus for K8S-PRIVILEGED-01 measurement. 6080 YAML files at SHA a716ee6f51c20672ca87e3e1ef1f05e02f56bea3 (sparse-checkout extended 2026-04-29). Same clone as iac-kubernetes-v1 (Dockerfile role) but YAML scope.

sources.name: kubernetes
sources.scope: *.yaml and *.yml files anywhere in repo (production-context filter applied)
sources.notes: Same clone as iac-kubernetes-v1; sparse-checkout extended to YAML 2026-04-29.

suitability.suitability_reason: Largest available k8s-manifest corpus. 6080 YAML files clear sample floor by orders of magnitude after production-context filter applies. Note: §2.4 production-context filter does not enumerate kubernetes monorepo test-artifact path conventions (staging/src/<repo>/<component>/artifacts/); v2 candidate.

limitations: Many tests/ and e2e/ subdirs; production-context filter expected to suppress aggressively.
§2.4 corpus-modeling gap on staging/src/.../artifacts/ inflates TP counts on operator-deferred-decision shapes; documented in MISSING-LIMITS and MISSING-PROBES v1 closeouts.
